{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/6476","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/6476/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/6476/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/6476/events","html_url":"https://github.com/astropy/astropy/issues/6476","id":252499596,"node_id":"MDU6SXNzdWUyNTI0OTk1OTY=","number":6476,"title":"`Time` parses fractional days in year-month-day format incorrectly","user":{"login":"evertrol","id":735460,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjczNTQ2MA==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/735460?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/evertrol","html_url":"https://github.com/evertrol","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/evertrol/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/evertrol/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/evertrol/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/evertrol/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/evertrol/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/evertrol/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/evertrol/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/evertrol/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/evertrol/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":10515381,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMDUxNTM4MQ==","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/labels/time","name":"time","color":"c7def8","default":false,"description":null},{"id":16191481,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxNjE5MTQ4MQ==","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/labels/Bug","name":"Bug","color":"d93f0b","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":5,"created_at":"2017-08-24T06:26:46Z","updated_at":"2022-09-21T14:24:10Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"`Time('2017-08-24.25')` results in `2017-08-24 00:00:00.250`: the fractional days are interpreted as fractional seconds (`2017-08-24 06:00:00` is what I hoped for).

The format `2017-08-24.25` is perhaps not the best format, but it is used, and since Astropy does not raise an exception, but silently returns an incorrect result, this may lead to errors.

The issue can be traced to `astropy.time.formats.TimeString().parse_string()`, which will interpret anything right of the last dot as a fractional second.
Since matching to regexes or `strptime` formats is done afterwards, there is no (easy) way to catch this through a subformat before the fractional second get stripped.

I'd be happy to try and put in a PR for this (if it's indeed a bug), but I'll need to know whether to raise an exception, or implement a proper parser for this format (provided it doesn't clash with other interpretations).
Some suggestions on the best way to attack this issue (or at what point in the code) are welcome as well.
","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/6476/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/6476/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/comments/1253788426","html_url":"https://github.com/astropy/astropy/issues/6476#issuecomment-1253788426","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/6476","id":1253788426,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAB_CCc5Ku0sK","user":{"login":"Mikea1985","id":14901128,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE0OTAxMTI4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/14901128?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Mikea1985","html_url":"https://github.com/Mikea1985","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Mikea1985/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Mikea1985/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Mikea1985/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Mikea1985/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Mikea1985/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Mikea1985/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Mikea1985/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Mikea1985/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Mikea1985/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2022-09-21T14:24:09Z","updated_at":"2022-09-21T14:24:09Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"This has caught me out a few times as well. The Minor Planet Center (MPC) uses a specific format for observations of asteroids and comets:
`'2020 08 15.59280'`
Which isn't understood by astropy.time.Time, but if spaces are replaced with dashes, it gives:
```
Time('2020 08 15.59280'.replace(' ', '-'))
<Time object: scale='utc' format='iso' value=2020-08-15 00:00:00.593>
```
whereas it should in fact convert to
`'2020-08-15 14:13:37.920'`
The best solution I have found is to add the decimal after converting to a Time object:
```
>>> Time('2020 08 15'.replace(' ', '-'))+'.59280'
<Time object: scale='utc' format='iso' value=2020-08-15 14:13:37.920>
```
But this is somewhat clunky. It would be nice if "mpc" (or "mpc_obs80") could be added to the allowed formats, so that I'd just need to remember to add the correct format specifier instead of changing spaces to dashes and adding the decimal day after the conversion to a Time object. 

(I work at the MPC, and my research also uses MPC-formatted files extensively, so I often come across this problem and finally decided to go raise an issue about it; I found several already open, so I just added to this one.)","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/comments/1253788426/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

2018-01-13T4:39:54.3 
would be:
2018 01 13.194378

Does astropy do this?

Thanks!","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/7133/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/7133/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/comments/1253792071","html_url":"https://github.com/astropy/astropy/issues/7133#issuecomment-1253792071","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/7133","id":1253792071,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAB_CCc5Ku1lH","user":{"login":"Mikea1985","id":14901128,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE0OTAxMTI4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/14901128?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Mikea1985","html_url":"https://github.com/Mikea1985","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Mikea1985/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Mikea1985/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Mikea1985/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Mikea1985/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Mikea1985/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Mikea1985/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Mikea1985/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Mikea1985/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Mikea1985/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2022-09-21T14:26:58Z","updated_at":"2022-09-21T14:26:58Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"I don't think there is a way to vote for issues, so I just wanted to add here that I would also enjoy if I could more easily parse/emit MPC formatted times into/out of an astropy.time.Time The beauty of the mean/median padding options is that it's not a constant padding, but the padding is the average of a given row/column, which is more desirable in some cases (like for the image processing that I'm doing; unfortunately, some of the images have nan values).","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/1233015578/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
